description: The textbook predator of warm, weedy, muddy-bottomed ponds. Greenish body with a dark horizontal stripe; upper jaw extends past the eye. Ambush feeder around lily pads, submerged wood, and weed edges.

Prefers cooler, clearer, rockier water than its largemouth cousin. Bronze-brown body with faint vertical bars and often a reddish eye. Upper jaw ends at the eye. A stretch species here — possible but less likely.
The classic panfish. Dark blue-black ear flap, orange-yellow belly in breeding males. Abundant in warm shallows and near structure; takes worms, crickets, and tiny poppers readily.
Brilliantly colored sunfish with orange-red spot on the ear flap and wavy blue lines on the cheek. Sticks close to weedy shorelines and quiet coves.
Golden-yellow body with 6–8 dark vertical bars. Schooling fish often found near drop-offs and submerged weeds. A reliable bite on small minnows and jigs.
Scaleless mud-dweller with long whiskers (barbels) and a rounded tail. Yellow-brown mottled body. Bottom-feeder most active at dusk and after dark.
Chunky panfish of rocky structure, with red eyes and a dark spot on each scale forming rows of dots. Aggressive bite on crayfish imitations and small jigs around boulders.
Sleek torpedo-shaped ambush predator with a golden body and distinctive dark chain-link pattern. Haunts weedy shorelines, lily pads, and submerged wood. Sharp teeth — use a leader.
Silvery-black speckled panfish that schools around submerged wood in 10–12 ft holes. Light bite on small minnows and marabou jigs, best at dawn and dusk.
Slender minnow with a golden-olive back and silver-yellow sides. The primary baitfish in warm ponds — essentially guaranteed where bass and pickerel feed well.

Stout minnow-family fish with olive-brown back, silvery sides, and a dark lateral stripe; breeding males develop rosy flanks and tubercles on the snout. Unlike most minnows, they grow to 10–12 inches. A sign of moving water nearby — they live in the feeder creeks and outflows, and drift into ponds where current enters. Prized as bait for bass and pickerel.
Large, heavy-bodied bottom-rooter with coppery-gold scales and two pairs of barbels. Rolls and splashes in shallows — you'll know if they're here. Sensitive fighter on corn or doughbait.
